ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for McDonald's McCrispy branded product; closest match is fried chicken food product (FOODON_00002490).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKM (chicken meat, fried, breaded) used as closest defensible match for breaded fried chicken pieces; no branded McDonald's McCrispy code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept for this branded fast food fried chicken product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving consists of 2 pieces of Chicken McCrispy Signature (Non-Spicy), approximately 220 g total, as served at McDonald's Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken McNuggets and Fried Chicken — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods; cross-referenced with McDonald's Singapore published nutrition information for Chicken McCrispy
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods) and McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Guide
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-serving nutrition data for Chicken McCrispy on its official Singapore nutrition guide. Per-100g values are derived by dividing the published per-serving values (for a comparable 2-piece serving ~220g) by 2.2. USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for McDonald's fried chicken products was used to cross-validate macro ratios. Values represent the non-spicy Signature variant. Energy: ~247 kcal/100g; Protein: ~16.5g/100g; Fat: ~14.5g/100g; Carbohydrate: ~14.5g/100g. These are consistent with published McDonald's Singapore nutrition data for the McCrispy product line.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; breaded fried chicken analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for McDonald's Chicken McCrispy. GI estimated at 54 (low) based on analogous breaded fried chicken products from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables, which report GI values of 48–60 for breaded/battered fried chicken products. The wheat-flour batter contributes moderate glycemic carbohydrates. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (54 × 31.9) / 100 ≈ 17.2, rounded to 17.3 for a 220g serving providing approximately 31.9g carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg/100g; ~1056 mg per 2-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this product, as a single 2-piece serving provides approximately 1056 mg sodium, representing over 45% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg and substantially exceeding the stricter 1500 mg target for hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~54) and moderate glycemic load (~17 per serving) from wheat-flour batter
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this product with caution; the breaded batter contributes approximately 32g of carbohydrate per 2-piece serving with a low GI of 54, producing a moderate glycemic load that can raise postprandial blood glucose, and the high sodium content adds additional cardiovascular risk in this population.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and cholesterol content from deep-frying in battered coating
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this product, as a 2-piece serving delivers approximately 7g saturated fat, 0.4g trans fat, 121 mg cholesterol, and over 1000 mg sodium, all of which are established risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken McCrispy (2 pieces) Signature Non-Spicy is a McDonald's Singapore fast food product consisting of two pieces of whole-muscle chicken coated in a thick, extra-crispy wheat-flour batter and deep-fried in vegetable oil. The non-spicy Signature variant uses a lightly seasoned, neutral-flavoured batter that produces a distinctively crunchy exterior while retaining juicy chicken meat inside. It is certified Halal and is a popular menu item at McDonald's outlets across Singapore. Nutritionally, each 2-piece serving (~220g) provides approximately 543 kcal, 36g protein, 32g fat, and 32g carbohydrate, making it a calorie-dense, high-protein, high-fat fast food item. The sodium content is notably high at approximately 1056 mg per serving, raising concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The breaded batter contributes a low glycemic load, relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The product is ultra-processed and not suitable for frequent consumption in the context of a heart-healthy or sodium-restricted diet. It contains gluten (wheat) as a major allergen and is dairy-free. The product is sold at McDonald's Singapore restaurants and is available via field survey delivery.
common_user_queries: How many calories in McDonald's Chicken McCrispy 2 pieces?
Is McDonald's McCrispy halal in Singapore?
Nutrition facts for Chicken McCrispy Singapore
Is Chicken McCrispy good for diabetics?
How much sodium in McDonald's McCrispy?

confidence_reason: McDonald's Singapore publishes nutrition information for the Chicken McCrispy product line on its official Singapore website and nutrition guide. Per-100g values were derived by normalising the published per-serving data for a 2-piece serving. USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods was used for cross-validation of macro ratios. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for breaded fried chicken, as no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists. Confidence is medium rather than high because the per-100g values are derived from serving-level data with an assumed serving weight, and GI is estimated rather than directly measured.
notes: 1. Per-100g nutrient values are derived from McDonald's Singapore published nutrition data for the Chicken McCrispy (2-piece serving, estimated ~220g total weight) normalised to 100g. Serving weight of 220g is an estimate based on comparable McDonald's fried chicken products; actual weight may vary slightly. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition guide or USDA Branded Foods cross-reference for this product. 3. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in the available sources for this product. 4. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition guide for this product. 5. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the available sources for this product. 6. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the available sources for this product. 7. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the available sources for this product. 8. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the available sources for this product. 9. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are all -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition guide or the USDA Branded Foods cross-reference for this product. 10. water_g is -1 as it is not reported in the available sources. 11. GI of 54 is an analogous estimate based on breaded fried chicken data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables; no direct GI measurement exists for this branded product. 12. The food is certified Halal by McDonald's Singapore. 13. The aliases_by_language hindi entry uses Devanagari script: चिकन मैक्क्रिस्पी (2 टुकड़े) सिग्नेचर नॉन-स्पाइसी — this exact string appears in the flat aliases list.
